# notification-channels

An alert delivery destination (email or Slack webhook), the target alerts land on, not the signal that detects nor the rule that decides what fires. Dashboard: Settings -> Notifications.

**When to use:** Wiring up, listing, editing, or test-firing where alerts get delivered (email or Slack).

> **Footgun:** Creating a channel is CLI-doable, but binding a signal to it as a firing alert rule is dashboard-only (no `alert-rules` group).

## Commands

| Command | Invocation | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `create` | `bentolabs notification-channels create --data '<json>'` | add an email or Slack channel (Slack needs config.webhook_url) |
| `delete` | `bentolabs notification-channels delete <channel_id>` | remove a channel |
| `get` | `bentolabs notification-channels get <channel_id>` | read one channel by id |
| `get-last-delivery` | `bentolabs notification-channels get-last-delivery <channel_id>` | status of the latest real delivery (vs test, which sends a new one) |
| `list` | `bentolabs notification-channels list` | channels in the workspace |
| `test` | `bentolabs notification-channels test <channel_id>` | send a fresh probe delivery now |
| `update` | `bentolabs notification-channels update <channel_id> --data '<json>'` | toggle on/off, rename, or rotate the webhook |

Confirm exact arguments with `bentolabs notification-channels <command> --help`.
